description: A corporate security team seized a USB drive from an employee suspected of exfiltrating proprietary data. You must determine how many distinct times the suspect copied files to this drive. Your mission: (1) Create a forensic case named 'Copy_Session_Analysis_2024' with case number 'INV-CSA-007' in Autopsy. (2) Add /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd as a Disk Image data source. For ingest modules, enable 'File Type Identification' and 'Recent Activity'; disable all others. Run ingest to completion. (3) Examine the file system tree to inspect file creation timestamps for all regular files. (4) Identify copy sessions: group files whose creation timestamps fall within a 120-second window of each other. A file belongs to a session if its creation time is within 120 seconds of the previous file in that chronological sequence. Isolated files are single-file sessions. (5) Write a session report to /home/ga/Reports/copy_sessions.txt with exactly these header lines: 'TOTAL_FILES_ANALYZED: <N>', 'TOTAL_SESSIONS: <N>', 'LARGEST_SESSION_FILE_COUNT: <N>'. Then, for each session (numbered from 1), include a block: 'SESSION <N>: <start> to <end> | FILES: <count>', followed by 'FILE: <filename> | PATH: <full_path> | CREATED: <timestamp> | SIZE: <bytes> | STATUS: <ALLOCATED|DELETED>' for each file in it. (6) Write an investigative summary to /home/ga/Reports/session_summary.txt containing these exact labels on separate lines: 'INVESTIGATION_SUBJECT:', 'TOTAL_COPY_SESSIONS:', 'SINGLE_FILE_SESSIONS:', 'MULTI_FILE_SESSIONS:', 'LARGEST_SESSION:', and 'CONCLUSION:'.
